meta.title: An Allocator's View: A Fireside Chat With a Major European Bank

transcript:
Fabio Massellani: Hey guys, good afternoon, very nice to meet you. Let me just drop this water here. So, very nice to be here, big honor. Um, let me just start before I start questioning Roman here, by raising your hand, how many of you have either a private banker or a financial advisor? One? No takers? Two? How many else? Ah yeah, I thought so. I thought so. And that's why? Because you guys are the tip of the spear. Many times during this day, this morning and then this afternoon, we spoke about institutional adoptions, right? And what I wanted to bring you today with Roman, if you guys will, is a perspective from the front lines. What does it mean to have institutional adoptions across cryptos and specifically on the Solana proper? And today we have with us Roman who is actually one of the pioneers in this respect. So, maybe Roman, how about we start with like a bit of an introduction about yourself and about your work at A&G?

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Thank you, Fabio. It seems that we have many of future clients over here, not many of them are quite advised. So yeah, I work in a bank. I am the crypto guy inside the bank. Almost to be fired a couple of times, as you can imagine. So I spend half of my day trying to convince really serious institutional investors, uh, not convince, just to explain what is really crypto and why they should allocate at least two to three percent in their asset allocation. And the other part of the day, I spend trying to convince our compliance team that I am not here to blow up the bank. So, uh, yeah, in fact, there is a funny story about this, Fabio, I want to tell you about because there was this one of the guys, one of our compliance officers, this was the one that was in charge to review all the presentations about our crypto product and make sure that we were really compliant with regulators. And after weeks of all this work, he came to me like, 'Okay Roman, after doing this due diligence, how can I invest in crypto?' So he finally invested. So the beauty about this is the people that really are digging in, that are doing the due diligence, are finally investing in crypto. So yeah, we finally launched this Criptomonedas fund. It's a crypto hedge fund based in Madrid, Spain, in 2023. It's the first liquid, daily liquid fund in Europe. It's 100% regulated by the Spanish authority and we are investing up to 100% in crypto using European physically backed ETPs.

Fabio Massellani: Love it. Thank you, Roman. Very nice. Um, actually I really planned to ask you how the idea for Criptomonedas came along because Criptomonedas is actually the first fund of its kind in the whole of Spanish-speaking investment space, which is amazing. Can you tell us a little bit more how the conversation was within the bank in order to get into this pioneering position? Because it's quite interesting. On one hand, it's more like, okay, how can we make sure that the investment, sorry, that the investor journey is safe, right, and we're compliant with regulation? And on the other hand, it's like, let's be innovative. Let's try to be ahead of the competition and launch something new and include crypto into our offering. How was that? What did you have to balance within the different, let's say, um, interests?

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Yeah, so the journey was about educating people. So first of all, there were client demand, okay? So young clients, they wanted exposure. So they were explaining their dads about crypto and they trying to convince them that's quite interesting. Uh, older clients were about, okay, maybe it's good to diversify a little bit. And all our private bankers and advisors were more about, okay, I need a product, a serious one where I can really offer this new asset class to my clients without sweating, a product what I can really defend into investment committee. So that's the reason we started thinking about creating this fund. Just to make a really proper regulated product, a serious one to invest in crypto for the long term.

Fabio Massellani: Got you. Absolutely. And then you mentioned specifically crypto ETPs, which is quite interesting. Like, um, can you tell us a little bit more, you know, why did you decide to build it, build this, your investment structure using these vehicles? Like what's so special about them? What makes them specifically fit to your use case?

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Yeah, I've been in crypto 10 plus years and sometimes I'm really well criticized in the, let's say, only crypto atmosphere like, okay, why ETPs? You know, let's try to do all kind of transaction and so on. I've done that and I still am doing that in Ethereum and Solana and all the layers. And, uh, I have to say that for the first 1,000 USD it's okay, maybe a couple of thousand it's okay, but when you try to invest more than 10,000, 100k into the space, you should go safe. I am a computer engineer and even myself sometimes I'm kind of like, I could mess it up with the keys, with the ledger, with everything. You know, it's like operational risk, uh, a lot of kind of phishing attempts. So, uh, it's so secure an ETF in the States or ETP in Europe to get involved in crypto that we really think that's the institutional and serious way for people to invest for the long term serious amounts of capital.

Fabio Massellani: Got you. Absolutely. Um, and actually to your point, just the feeling of safety is so fundamental for in the end the people who are the beneficial owners in then your funds, right? They have to benefit from that type of custodial security, custodial safety. So very important point. Um, let me ask you a little bit, um, so you talked to us about how the Criptomonedas idea kind of came to be, what you had to, let's say, negotiate in terms of internal, uh, stakeholders, let's say, in your strategy. Tell us a little bit about your work, your strategy specifically if you can. I don't want to reveal any family secrets, just what you feel like sharing with us.

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Yeah, Fabio, the strategy is simple. I mean, this space is going to give you 40% annualized returns for the next 10 years in my opinion. So it's kind of just stay focused, diversify, be really blockchain agnostic, although you know, uh, we are here for Solana today, but I mean, you have to take into account that there are several major ones and you should allocate to them properly. So for example, uh, what we call the fat protocols, Bitcoin, Ether, and SOL, uh, those ones, you know, the... they are huge in comparison with the other ones. With that it means I like to compare it to mobile, uh, smartphones, uh, operating systems. There are Android, there is iOS, and nothing more. Even Microsoft tried it with Windows Phone. You know anybody with Windows Phone here? Nobody. So I mean, same is happening with these protocols. I mean, when you have in the smart contract protocols landscape protocols like Ethereum or Solana, the cost of penetration of new protocols is so high that you should allocate properly. That's the reason we have higher percentage and higher allocation in our fund to Bitcoin, Ether, and SOL, and fewer percentages to other protocols that, yeah, maybe they could go on, uh, a lot, they could grow a lot, but the probability to fell apart in a four or five year period, it's pretty high.

Fabio Massellani: Got you. Absolutely, yeah. And, um, I love also the idea that you're sharing with us, the idea of giving market access to the investor, right? Like the real benefit being you're in the space, you're investing in this space, doesn't necessarily specifically accrue to one asset. But since we're here, since we're at Solana Breakpoint, let me ask you something more about Solana. What did you get... So first of all, are you a strategic investor in the fund? You just mentioned it, but how did you get to take that decision and, um, how's it gone for your investment journey so far?

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Yeah, so, yes, Solana... I think I'm the first one that is telling you this here. Solana fell apart more than 97% in the last bear market in 2022. So I mean, the resilience that Solana show us really surprised. The recovery of Solana, of the protocol, was pretty high in my opinion. There are few coins that if you measure the price action in terms of Bitcoin, for example, there are few ones that recover so fast as Solana did. So that's the reason we had a 4% allocation at the beginning of the fund. And later on, we saw a really increase in fundamentals in the network. So a lot of developer activity, a lot of application, real application, real blockchain application growing on the protocol. So that's the reason we really dig into our due diligence, we really dig into the protocol, you know all the boring due diligence stuff, and we finally decide to allocate up to 10% into Solana. So that's our allocation, uh, during this, uh, you know, uh, drop in the alt... there is no alt season, as you know that. And that's the reason we are, uh, you know...

Fabio Massellani: You were the first to say it today.

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Yeah, but we are overweighting now Solana because in terms of market dominance, I think it's around three point something, but, uh, I mean we are... we have triple amount of weight in the protocol. So our current allocation is 10% in the fund.

Fabio Massellani: Oh perfect. Got you. So it's interesting because you're overweighting Solana even despite what the metrics tell you.

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Yeah, even 10% maybe could seem pretty low, you know, for a Solana ecosystem network event. I have to tell you that it's a lot in terms of market dominance.

Fabio Massellani: Absolutely. Thank you very much. And let me just follow up still on a question about Solana because I know that you followed this quite specifically. What are the drivers that you see going onwards for your... that are of interest to you as an asset allocator for the Solana ecosystem that would make you say, 'Oh actually, that's somewhere that I really want to be in, that's somewhere that excites me and I want to participate into that, making sure that my investors participate into that'?

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Yeah, our coin investment decision is pretty boring. I mean, you don't have to be the most coolest new protocol or new crypto. You only have to survive a morning meeting risk committee in the morning. So, uh, yeah, I mean, not funny stuff. We look for really key fundamentals. Uh, we are looking for real cryptocurrencies, so permissionless is really important for us. Uh, decentralization, sometimes they argue that Solana isn't decentralized enough. I would tell them that, uh, even a Solana financial ecosystem is better than the current financial traditional rails. So talking about that, uh, we saw a really huge explosion in stablecoin worlds. As you know, more than 300 billion are running now in stablecoins around the world and it's growing a lot in networks like Solana. So I mean, when you are running a stablecoin and you are moving USDC, USDT around the world, you already have a counterparty risk into Circle or Tether. So I mean, maybe it's not really worry about the level of decentralization of Solana. It's more about how Solana is really fast, how Solana is allowing you to move dollars at the speed of light. And it's converting dollars into programmable money. So I think that's pretty interesting in terms of which crypto we want to have in our portfolio.

Fabio Massellani: Absolutely. Thank you very much. And a very last thing, very last minute. I opened by asking a specific question about our industry, right? Our industry is like any other industry, has a top and a bottom and it has a distribution functions. And, um, and I think what excites me about it is that very many times you have to educate people. It's still in a point, at least in my country, but I suppose in Spain it's similar as well. Um, and specifically in your case, you finally have something to offer your guys in order to get out there and respond to client demand. What do you guys do in terms of education towards your network, towards your private bankers and towards the broader activity in order to spread the word?

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Yeah, it's a lot of meetings with people trying to explain what is crypto, how they should get involved, and how they really should have that 1.5, 2% structural positioning in crypto because this is an asset class that decorrelate. So sometimes it's difficult because it's seen as a lottery or something like that from outside, but it's not. I mean, this is going to give you 40% annualized returns and that's going to review your capital by 30 times. So it's not about if, it's about how much to allocate. So at least 1% in my opinion.

Fabio Massellani: Amazing. Thank you very much. Thanks Roman.

Roman Gonzalez Torres: Thank you all.

sources.title: Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management
sources.snippet:
Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in AUM
# Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management
San Francisco •Nov 21, 2025
![Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) Surpasses $500M in Assets Under Management](https://bitwiseinvestments.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.datocms-assets.com%2F62087%2F1762788442-bsol-bitwise-solana-staking-etf-press-500m-aum.png&w=3840&q=75)
Bitwise Asset Management, the global crypto asset manager with over $15 billion in client assets, announced today that the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) has surpassed $500 million in assets under management (AUM), achieving the landmark figure in its first 18 days of trading.
"We are truly in awe of the speed and scale of BSOL’s growth," said Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley. "Crypto is becoming a mainstream asset class. BSOL hitting half a billion dollars in AUM this quickly is a clear testament to investors’ belief in the Solana ecosystem as a cornerstone of the future of the crypto industry."
As one of the first crypto ETPs with built-in staking, BSOL aims to stake 100% of the Fund’s SOL holdings through Bitwise Onchain Solutions, powered by Helius, the leading Solana staking technology known for its high performance, technical excellence, and security. The strategy seeks to maximize investors’ participation in Solana’s average staking rewards of over 7%.¹
BSOL has a net sponsor fee of 0.00% for the first three months on its first $1 billion in assets. All staking fees are also waived for the first three months. After three months, the sponsor fee will be 0.20% and the staking fee, which applies only to the staking rewards that BSOL earns, will be 0.06%.
###### **Note:**
###### (1) Staking reward rate is based on the annualized 90-day average as of 11/20/25, with data from Helius. Rewards are subject to change and should not be considered an indication of the Fund’s performance. The tax treatment of rewards earned by the Trust may have potentially adverse consequences for shareholders. Please consult with your tax advisor.
### Risks and Important Information
**This material must be accompanied by a prospectus. Please read the prospectus carefully before investing. To obtain a current prospectus visit**[**bsoletf.com/welcome**](https://bsoletf.com/welcome)**.**
**The Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) is not suitable for all investors. An investment in BSOL is subject to a high degree of risk, has the potential for significant volatility, and could result in significant or complete loss of investment. BSOL is not an investment company registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended (the “1940 Act”) and therefore is not subject to the same protections as ETFs and mutual funds registered under the 1940 Act. An investment in BSOL is not the same as a direct investment in Solana (SOL).**
Shares of ETPs are bought and sold at market price (not NAV) and are not individually redeemed from the Fund. Brokerage commissions will reduce returns. The NAV may not always correspond to the market price of SOL and, as a result, Creation Units may be created or redeemed at a value that is different from the market price of the Shares. Authorized Participants’ buying and selling activity associated with the creation and redemption of Creation Units may adversely affect an investment in the Shares.
The amount of SOL represented by a Share will continue to be reduced during the life of the Fund due to the transfer of the Fund’s SOL to pay for the Sponsor’s management fee, and to pay for litigation expenses or other extraordinary expenses. This dynamic will occur irrespective of whether the trading price of the Shares rises or falls in response to changes in the price of SOL.
There is no guarantee or assurance that the Fund’s methodology will result in the Fund achieving positive investment returns or outperforming other investment products.
Investors may choose to use the Fund as a means of investing

sources.title: Grayscale Waives Management Fees and Expands Staking up to 100% for Solana Trust (GSOL), Offering 7.23% Staking Rewards Rate

Grayscale Waives Management Fees and Expands Staking up to

# [![Grayscale_G_Blackberry.png](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/4b944bce-637f-4386-ac51-330819cae5f3?size=3)](https://grayscale.com/)
# Grayscale Waives Management Fees and Expands Staking up to 100% for Solana Trust (GSOL), Offering 7.23% Staking Rewards Rate
## Product enhancements, real-time staking rewards, and diversified validators help position GSOL as cost-efficient, transparent Solana ETP
November 05, 2025 09:00 ET| Source:[Grayscale Investments](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/Grayscale%20Investments)FollowGrayscale Investments
Share
* * * * * * * * STAMFORD, Conn., Nov. 05, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Grayscale Investments®, the world’s largest digital asset-focused investment platform\*, today announced that it has waived the sponsor's fee and reduced the fees associated with staking for Grayscale Solana Trust ETF (Ticker: GSOL) for up to three months, or until the fund’s AUM reaches $1 billion, whichever occurs first. The fund is also now staking up to 100% of its SOL at a 7.23% Staking Reward Rate\*\*, and the fee waiver applies to both new and existing GSOL investors.\*\*\*
*GSOL, an exchange traded product, is not registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 (the “40 Act”) and therefore is not subject to the same regulations and protections as 40 Act-registered ETFs and mutual funds. An investment in GSOL involves significant risk, including possible loss of principal. GSOL holds Solana; however, an investment in GSOL is not a direct investment in Solana.*
“By waiving the management fee and reducing the staking fee for GSOL, we’re directing more of the economics to investors,” said Inkoo Kang, Senior Vice President, ETFs, at Grayscale. “We have been staking in GSOL since October 6th, even before it became an ETP. GSOL aims to deliver real long-term benefits for investors, highlighted by our diversified validator approach, a key aspect of the staking program deployed in GSOL.”
Solana is a high-performance, proof-of-stake blockchain known for high throughput and low fees, fueling adoption from new users to institutions. GSOL offers exchange-listed exposure to Solana and live staking rewards via a leading staking program, now with a temporary waiver of the sponsor's fee and reduced staking fee to further benefit shareholders.
GSOL launched in 2021 as a private placement, listed on OTCQX in 2023, and in October 2025 began staking and uplisted as an ETP shortly thereafter. Grayscale stakes up to 100% of the fund, offering investors the potential to compound over time.
For additional information about GSOL, please visit:[https://etfs.grayscale.com/gsol](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=16eqWSY1y9Sa6QZhdKhmLwyoNlhn3JbMXzEQbc8obtCAHpVJJs79j-ZEvbGpUgWtEJRkGLqrSgRkx4btfW4xIW4haPMnRA9Xg7HCRjga9xl3rCY8zFCAHyR_cjgq-Tnu)
**About****Grayscale**
Grayscale enables investors to access the digital economy through a family of future-forward investment products. Founded in 2013, Grayscale has a decade-long track record and deep expertise as a digital asset-focused investment platform. Investors, advisors, and allocators turn to Grayscale for single asset, diversified, and thematic exposure. For more information, please follow @Grayscale or visit grayscale.com.
\*Largest digital asset-focused investment platform based on AUM as of 11/3/2025. For other companies in this category, AUM is considered as of most recent public disclosure.
\*\*Gross staking return of 7.23% represents the average total annualized return on st

sources.title: VanEck Debuts Solana ETF: VSOL Launches with Zero Fees
sources.snippet:
-

# VanEck Debuts Solana ETF: VSOL Launches with Zero Fees

Share

_VSOL sponsor fees will be waived for the first $1 billion in AUM from November 17, 2025 through February 17, 2026. Thereafter, the sponsor fee will be 0.30%. Brokerage fees and commissions may apply. Please check with your broker._

**_Investments in the_** [**_VanEck Bitcoin ETF (“HODL”)_**](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2Finvestments%2Fbitcoin-etf-hodl%2Fhodl-prospectus.pdf&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck+Bitcoin+ETF+%28%26%238220%3BHODL%26%238221%3B%29&index=1&md5=a1ac288b86eae8c1b2c7e4841a392e1d) **_, the_** [**_VanEck Solana ETF (“VSOL”)_**](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2Finvestments%2Fsolana-etf-vsol%2Fvsol-prospectus.pdf&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck+Solana+ETF+%28%26%238220%3BVSOL%26%238221%3B%29&index=2&md5=fc24029d3ccd5909f6991b0ad6485c73) **_, and the_** [**_VanEck Ethereum ETF (“ETHV”)_**](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2Finvestments%2Fethereum-etf-ethv%2Fethv-prospectus.pdf&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck+Ethereum+ETF+%28%26%238220%3BETHV%26%238221%3B%29&index=3&md5=11372d0c9d11a3d933cf8e06e11653fb) **_(collectively, the “Trusts”) are subject to significant risk and may not be suitable for all investors. The values of Bitcoin, Solana, and Ethereum are highly volatile, and you can lose your entire principal investment. The Trusts are not investment companies registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940 (the “1940 Act”) and therefore are not subject to the same protections as mutual funds or ETFs registered under the 1940 Act._**

NEW YORK--( [BUSINESS WIRE](https://www.businesswire.com))-- [VanEck](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2F%3Futm_source%3Dpr%26utm_medium%3Dpressrelease&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck&index=4&md5=de0d0ef9cac5fe95fb253faee1b528ee) is introducing the [VanEck Solana ETF (VSOL)](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vaneck.com%2Fus%2Fen%2Finvestments%2Fsolana-etf-vsol%2F&esheet=54358954&newsitemid=20251117326608&lan=en-US&anchor=VanEck+Solana+ETF+%28VSOL%29&index=5&md5=1ca2664152b054cacfa5fb8c3e29c91f), offering investors exposure to SOL, the native token of the Solana network, and to the staking rewards earned for helping secure the network (please see the disclosures below for information related to staking).

> “We’re excited to be launching VSOL and to build on VanEck’s long history of expanding access to digital assets through thoughtful, investor-focused products,” Kyle DaCruz, Director, Digital Assets Product with VanEck.
>
> Share

VanEck is waiving its sponsor fee for VSOL at launch for the first $1 billion in assets or until February 17, 2026, whichever comes first. During this same period, VSOL’s third-party staking service provider will also waive its fee for staking services. If VSOL’s assets exceed $1 billion before February 17, 2026, a 0.30% sponsor fee will apply to assets above that threshold. After February 17, 2026, the sponsor fee will be 0.30%.

Solana’s historical high-performance, low-cost architecture has made it a leading blockchain for onchain activity, processing tens of millions of transactions per day across decentralized finance (DeFi), gaming, NFTs, and tokenized real-world assets. Built for scalability and efficiency, Solana combines a Proof of History (PoH) mechanism with Proof of Stake (PoS) consensus, enabling fast block times and low transaction costs. Validators stake SOL to verify transactions, secure the network, and earn staking rewards in return.

tags.impact_reason: A major European bank allocating to crypto, specifically Solana, signals growing institutional acceptance and could lead to further adoption.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a classic, heavy bank vault as the semantic anchor because it powerfully represents the traditional, conservative world of Spanish private banking (A&G). The story's core is this institution's pioneering leap into crypto, so I depicted the vault being opened to reveal a glowing Solana network inside, symbolizing the 'disruption from within'. For the diegetic text, I integrated '10%' onto a sleek, digital screen on the vault's locking mechanism. This number is the specific 'high-conviction allocation' that makes the story newsworthy, directly linking the visual to the key data point.
visual_idea.description: A low-angle shot of a colossal, circular bank vault door made of brushed steel and brass, set within a dark, opulent marble hall. The vault door is slightly ajar, and a brilliant, volumetric purple and green light—the colors of Solana—spills out, cutting through the shadows. On the intricate central locking mechanism, a small, modern, integrated digital screen glows with the stark text '10%'. Through the crack, instead of gold, we see a glimpse of a crystalline, glowing network of data, representing the 'fat protocol'.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A low-angle shot of a colossal, circular bank vault door made of brushed steel and brass, set within a dark, opulent marble hall. The vault door is slightly ajar, and a brilliant, volumetric purple and green light—the vibrant colors of Solana—spills out, dramatically cutting through the deep shadows, illuminating dust motes in the air. On the intricate central locking mechanism, a small, modern, integrated digital screen glows intensely with the stark numerical text '10%', the digits appearing to be physically rendered light within the screen. Through the narrow crack, instead of traditional gold, a mesmerizing glimpse of a crystalline, glowing network of intricate data is revealed, representing a 'fat protocol', rendered with a luminous, ethereal quality. Vertical composition, emphasizing immense height and epic scale, with ample negative space above the monumental vault door to highlight its grandeur. The scene is imbued with a pioneer aesthetic, showcasing cutting-edge technology within a classic, imposing structure. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, volumetric lighting, subtle particle glow, highly realistic.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a classic, heavy bank vault as the semantic anchor because it powerfully represents the traditional, conservative world of Spanish private banking (A&G). The story's core is this institution's pioneering leap into crypto, so I depicted the vault being opened to reveal a glowing Solana network inside, symbolizing the 'disruption from within'. For the diegetic text, I integrated '10%' onto a sleek, digital screen on the vault's locking mechanism. This number is the specific 'high-conviction allocation' that makes the story newsworthy, directly linking the visual to the key data point.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on the complex, interlocking gears and tumblers of the steel bank vault's locking mechanism. The image is bisected by a sharp crack where the vault is open, from which an intense, ethereal purple and green light emanates, glinting off the polished metal surfaces. The focus is on the textural contrast between the heavy, cold steel of tradition and the energetic, luminous glow of the new technology seeping out.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro shot, ultra-detailed, focusing intently on the intricate, interlocking gears and precision-engineered tumblers of a robust, heavy brushed steel bank vault's locking mechanism. The scene is bisected by a sharp, jagged crack where the vault is ajar, from which an intense, ethereal purple and vibrant green light emanates, glowing with a luminous, crystalline quality. This vibrant light casts dramatic high-contrast reflections and glints across the polished, cold metal surfaces, highlighting every minute scratch and imperfection of the steel, while creating deep, moody shadows. The composition is perfectly square (1:1), symmetrical, with a central focus, tightly framed to fill the entire image with the rich textural contrast: the traditional, weighty, cold steel of tradition against the energetic, luminous glow of the new, pioneering technology seeping out. Volumetric lighting, High Dynamic Range (HDR), photorealistic, 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, microscopic details.

editorial.headline: Spanish Private Bank A&G Overweights Solana in Regulated Hedge Fund
editorial.standfirst: A&G’s Criptomonedas FIL defies conservative banking norms with a high-conviction 10% allocation to Solana. The move signals a shift in European institutional risk appetite, utilizing regulated ETP rails to capture "fat protocol" dominance.
key_takeaways: **10% Solana Allocation**: A&G’s Criptomonedas FIL holds triple the market-cap weight of SOL, citing developer resilience and stablecoin velocity.
**40% Annualized Target**: Fund manager Roman Gonzalez Torres projects a decade of 40% annualized returns, advising a 1-3% portfolio allocation for decorrelation.
**Regulated Rails**: Launched in 2023 under CNMV supervision, the fund offers daily liquidity via physically backed European ETPs to mitigate operational risk.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a landscape often dominated by passive index tracking, **A&G Bank**—a leading Spanish private bank—has taken a distinctively active stance with its **Criptomonedas FIL**. While many institutions limit exposure to Bitcoin and Ethereum, A&G has allocated **10% of the fund to Solana**, a weighting roughly triple the network's market dominance. Roman Gonzalez Torres, the fund's manager, cites the protocol’s resilience following the 2022 market collapse as a primary driver. Despite a 97% drawdown during that cycle, Solana’s developer retention and the explosion of real-world utility—specifically in stablecoin velocity and programmable money—validated the bank's high-conviction bet.

The fund’s strategy is rooted in a "Fat Protocol" thesis that likens Layer 1 blockchains to mobile operating systems. Just as the mobile market consolidated around Android and iOS, Gonzalez Torres argues the smart contract landscape is hardening around a duopoly of **Ethereum** and **Solana**. The cost of penetration for new protocols is becoming prohibitively high, creating a moat for established leaders. Consequently, Criptomonedas FIL concentrates capital in these winners rather than diluting returns across a long tail of "altcoins" with high probabilities of failure over a four-year cycle.

Structurally, A&G has solved the "institutional access" problem by utilizing **European physically backed ETPs** rather than direct on-chain custody. This approach mitigates the operational risks that terrify compliance departments—private key management, phishing, and ledger security—while preserving daily liquidity for investors. By wrapping high-beta assets in a regulated, CNMV-compliant vehicle, A&G provides a safe conduit for conservative capital to access what Gonzalez Torres projects as **40% annualized returns** over the next decade. For allocators, the message is clear: the risk is no longer in exposure, but in zero allocation.
